vocab.meaning: một sự thay đổi hoàn toàn và sâu sắc
vocab.example: The transition from classical portraiture to digital installations represents a sea change in how we define artistic expression.

vocab.word: out with the old, in with the new
vocab.ipa: /aʊt wɪð ði əʊld ɪn wɪð ðə njuː/
vocab.meaning: bỏ cái cũ, đón nhận cái mới
vocab.example: Modern art often follows the philosophy of 'out with the old, in with the new', discarding traditional realism for abstract concepts.

vocab.meaning: phần bổ sung làm cho mọi thứ hoàn hảo hơn
vocab.example: Traditional paintings are beautiful, but the historical context behind them is often the icing on the cake for art historians.

vocab.word: hit the nail on the head
vocab.meaning: diễn tả chính xác/đúng trọng tâm
vocab.example: Contemporary artists often hit the nail on the head when they use their work to critique modern social issues.
Many old masters were actually ahead of their time, experimenting with light in ways that inspired modern impressionism.

vocab.meaning: ở vị trí tiên phong, hiện đại nhất
vocab.example: Unlike traditional oil paintings, modern art is often at the cutting edge of technology, utilizing Phạm Tiến Dũng Gia Sư and virtual reality.

vocab.meaning: mô tả/tái hiện một cách sinh động
vocab.example: Classical artworks aim to paint a vivid picture of reality, whereas modern pieces often leave the interpretation to the viewer.

vocab.meaning: một luồng gió mới (sự thay đổi thú vị)
vocab.example: After seeing so many formal portraits, the vibrant colors of pop art felt like a breath of fresh air.
While old masters left their mark through technical perfection, modern artists do so through radical innovation.

vocab.meaning: kết hợp được ưu điểm của cả hai (cũ và mới)
vocab.example: Neo-classical art offers the best of both worlds, blending ancient themes with a more modern artistic sensibility.
